prs.title: fix: validate disconnected nodes and exclude input nodes from outputs
prs.body:
## Problem
Closes #244
Supersedes #245

Two bugs in model_generation.py silently broke model generation:

- Disconnected nodes caused a KeyError crash — BFS never visits 
  unconnected layers but the code still tries to access them in 
  keras_tensors

- Input nodes were misidentified as outputs — custominput nodes 
  with no outgoing edges were included in output_ids, producing 
  malformed Keras models silently

## Fix
- Added validation after BFS loop that raises a clear ValueError 
  listing exactly which nodes are disconnected
- Fixed output_ids filter to exclude custominput nodes

## Testing
- Disconnected canvas nodes → clear error message shown ✅
- Connected canvas → model generates correctly ✅
- Iris sample project still works perfectly ✅

## Note
Fresh PR as requested by @ivantha from #245.
Rebased on latest main, single clean commit, all checks passing.

prs.title: fix: replace yaml.safe_load with proper file read in code templates
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes an unnecessary `pyyaml` dependency and resource leak across all three generated code templates.

Fixes #285

## What was wrong

All three templates used `yaml.safe_load` to read a `.json` file:
```python
json_string = json.dumps(yaml.safe_load(open("model.json")))
```

This has three problems:

- **Wrong tool** — the file being read is JSON, not YAML. Using `pyyaml` to parse it is semantically incorrect.
- **Unnecessary dependency** — `pyyaml` only exists in `pyproject.toml` because of these three lines. Anyone who downloads a generated script and runs it locally would need to install `pyyaml` for no reason.
- **Resource leak** — calling `open()` without a `with` statement leaks file handles. This is exactly what ruff rule `SIM115` flags, which is why it was being suppressed for templates in `pyproject.toml`.

## What was fixed

Replaced with a proper context manager, matching exactly how `model_run.py` already reads the same file at runtime:
```python
with open("model.json") as f:
    json_string = f.read()
```

Also removed `SIM115` from the ruff suppression list in `pyproject.toml` since it is no longer needed.

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix

## How Has This Been Tested?

- Manually verified that `f.read()` and `json.dumps(yaml.safe_load(...))` produce identical output for valid JSON files
- Confirmed `model_run.py` uses the same pattern to load the identical file at runtime

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review
- [x] I have added/updated documentation as needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] Tests pass locally

prs.title: fix: add frontend validation for Dropout rate and Input layer dimensions
prs.body:
Part 2 for fixing #263.


Changes:
- **NodePropertiesPanel.jsx**: clamped Dropout rate to [0, 0.9]; enforced min=1 and int for Input layer dimensions
- **Helpers.jsx**: guarded `canSaveModel()` with input integer checks and dropout rate validations so models cannot be saved with invalid inputs

prs.title: fix: add backend support for Dropout layer node (customdropout)
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes #281

The frontend ships a complete Dropout layer UI — sidebar drag item, `DropoutNode`
component, and a properties panel with a rate input. The backend `_build_layer()` in
`model_generation.py` had no handler for `customdropout`, causing any model containing
a Dropout node to crash with **HTTP 500** rather than a helpful error.

The root cause is two-fold:
1. Missing `customdropout` branch in `_build_layer()`
2. The `model_generation()` call in both `model_validate_service` and
   `model_save_service` was outside `try/except`, so the `ValueError` escaped
   all error handling and hit the generic 500 handler

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/services/model_generation.py` | Add `customdropout` → `tf.keras.layers.Dropout`; validate rate ∈ [0, 1); default to 0.5 |
| `app/services/deep_learning.py` | Wrap `model_generation()` call in `try/except ValueError` in both service functions |
| `tests/test_model_generation.py` | 7 new tests: shape preservation, default rate, boundary values, invalid rates, full pipeline |

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix

## How Has This Been Tested?

- [x] Existing tests pass (`uv run pytest tests/test_model_generation.py -v`)
- [x] 7 new tests added covering all branches of the dropout handler
- [x] `uv run ruff check` passes with no warnings

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review
- [x] I have added/updated documentation as needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] Tests pass locally

prs.title: fix: raise ValidationError for disconnected nodes and exclude input nodes from outputs in model_generation.py
prs.body:
## Problem
Closes #244

Two critical bugs in model_generation.py silently broke 
model generation with no user-facing error:

1. Disconnected nodes cause KeyError crash — BFS never 
   visits unconnected layers but the code still tries to 
   access them in keras_tensors, causing a hard crash

2. Input nodes misidentified as outputs — custominput nodes 
   with no outgoing edges get included in output_ids, 
   producing malformed Keras models silently

## Fix

Added validation after BFS loop that raises a clear 
ValueError listing exactly which nodes are disconnected.

Fixed output_ids filter to exclude custominput nodes.

## Testing
- Disconnected canvas nodes → clear error message shown ✅
- Connected canvas → model generates correctly ✅
- Iris sample project still works perfectly ✅

prs.title: fix(security): add valibot override to resolve security vulnerabilities
prs.body:
fix(security): add valibot override to resolve security vulnerabilities

Add valibot >=1.2.0 override to pnpm.overrides to fix ReDoS vulnerability
in @prisma/dev transitive dependency (GHSA-vqpr-j7v3-hqw9).

The hono override (>=4.10.3) was already in place to address:
- Body Limit Middleware Bypass (GHSA-92vj-g62v-jqhh)
- Improper Authorization (GHSA-m732-5p4w-x69g)
- Vary Header Injection (GHSA-q7jf-gf43-6x6p)

Closes #28752

prs.title: feat: implement full Invite System with modal, dashboard, and API int…
prs.body:
###  Invite System Implementation

#### Key Features:
- Project owners can now **invite developers** from the Explore Users page
- Invites are stored in DB with status: `pending`, `accepted`, or `declined`
- Invited users have a new **"My Invites"** page to view and respond

#### Backend APIs:
- `POST /api/invites` → Send an invite with projectId, receiverId, role
- `GET /api/invites/received?userId=...` → Fetch invites received by a user
- `PATCH /api/invites/:id` → Accept or decline an invite
####  Frontend:
- Added `InviteModal.jsx` to allow project owners to select a project and assign a role
- Updated `UserCard.jsx` to trigger invite modal
- Created `MyInvites.jsx` page with accept/decline buttons
- Updated `AppRoutes.jsx` to register `/my-invites` route

####  Flow:
Send Invite → User Accept/Decline → Collaborators updated

prs.title: Show logged-in user's own projects on /my-projects page using Clerk ID
prs.body:
This feature enables users to view and manage only the projects they have personally created.

We use the logged-in user's Clerk ID to filter and fetch their projects via the `/api/projects?createdBy=clerkId` query.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Fetch projects based on the logged-in Clerk user ID
- Show a list of only those projects on the My Projects page
- Include UI for View and Edit Invite Status (no backend update yet)
- Display fallback UI if user has not posted any projects
- Optional: Prepare for adding "Post New Project" CTA

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * The "My Projects" page now dynamically fetches and displays projects associated with the logged-in user, replacing the previous static list.
  * Loading and empty states are shown appropriately during project fetching or when no projects are found.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Project cards now display the project title correctly and default invite status to "Pending" when not specified.
  * Invite status edits update the project list reliably without mutating data directly.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: feat: implement skill-based filtering and pagination for GET /api/users
prs.body:
This PR adds support for skill-based filtering and pagination on the GET /api/users endpoint.

### Changes
- Parses `stack` query param (?stack=react,node)
- Filters users who have at least one matching skill
- Adds pagination support with `page` and `limit` params
- Includes related skills in response with nested skill details

### Example usage:
GET /api/users?stack=react,node&page=1&limit=5

prs.title: Add more dummy projects for Explore Projects page
prs.body:
Added 3 new dummy projects to `dummyProjects.js` to enhance the Explore Projects page. This supports the display of multiple project cards with varied tech stacks, roles, and descriptions.

### Changes Made
- Expanded `dummyProjects` array to include 5 total projects.
- Ensured 4 out of 5 projects have `status: "Open for Collaboration"` for visibility on the Explore Projects page.
- Included diversity in tech stacks (React, Solidity, Flutter, etc.) and roles needed.

### Notes
These updates help simulate the final behavior until backend integration is complete. Each project can now be browsed and interacted with via the "Show Interest" button (console log for now).

issues.title: [Bug] Code templates use yaml.safe_load to read JSON files, causing unnecessary pyyaml dependency and resource leak
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The three generated code templates (linear-regression-all-float.py, multi-class-all-float-classification-csv.py, simple-image-classification.py). use yaml.safe_load to read a .json file, then re-serialize it with json.dumps. 
This is semantically incorrect since the file is JSON not YAML, introduces an unnecessary pyyaml dependency into every generated user script, and leaks a file handle by calling open() without a context manager.

**To Reproduce**
1. Build and save any model on the canvas
2. Click "Download Code" on the Training page
3. Open the downloaded .py file
4. Observe: import yaml is present and the model is loaded via json.dumps(yaml.safe_load(open("model.json")))
5. Attempting to run the script on a machine without pyyaml installed will fail with ModuleNotFoundError: No   module named 'yaml'

**Expected behavior**
The generated script should read the JSON file directly using a context 
manager, matching how model_run.py already loads the same file at runtime:

with open("model.json") as f:
    json_string = f.read()

No pyyaml import should be required.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots.

**Environment:**
- OS: All platforms
- Browser: Not applicable  
- Node.js version: Not applicable
- Python version: 3.12+

**Additional context**
The ruff rule SIM115 (use context manager for open()) was being suppressed 
in pyproject.toml specifically for templates to hide this issue. 
See PR #284 for the fix.

issues.title: [Bug] Dropout layer node causes HTTP 500 - backend missing customdropout handler
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The frontend ships a fully functional Dropout layer node (`customdropout` type) — 
it has a sidebar drag item, a `DropoutNode` component, and a properties panel with 
a rate slider. However `_build_layer()` in `model_generation.py` has no handler for 
this node type.

Additionally, the `model_generation()` call in both `model_validate_service` and 
`model_save_service` sits outside any `try/except`, so the resulting `ValueError` 
propagates unhandled and returns **HTTP 500** instead of a meaningful error message.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Open the canvas in any project
2. Drag a **Dropout** node from the sidebar onto the canvas
3. Connect it between two Dense layers
4. Click **Save Model**

**Expected:** Model saves successfully with dropout applied  
**Actual:** HTTP 500 — `{"success": false, "message": "Internal server error"}`

**Screenshots**
Not applicable - this is a backend error with no visual output.

**Environment:**
- OS: macOS Tahoe 26.2 (MacBook Pro M3 Pro)
- Browser: Not applicable (backend bug)
- Node.js version: Not applicable (backend bug)
- Python version: 3.13.12


**Additional context**
Confirmed by tracing the full call stack:

model_validate_service() → model_generation() → _build_layer()
→ raises ValueError("Unknown node type: customdropout")
→ unhandled (call sits outside try/except)
→ generic_exception_handler → HTTP 500

The frontend correctly sends type: "customdropout" — this is purely 
a missing backend handler. Fix is ready with 7 new passing tests.

issues.title: [Bug] Disconnected canvas nodes cause KeyError crash and input nodes misidentified as outputs in model_generation.py
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Two critical bugs in model_generation.py silently produce incorrect 
Keras models with no error shown to the user.

Bug 1 — KeyError crash on disconnected nodes:
If a user leaves any unconnected layer on the canvas, the BFS 
traversal never visits it. But the code still tries to look it 
up in keras_tensors, causing a hard KeyError crash with no 
helpful error message to the user.

Bug 2 — Input nodes misidentified as outputs:
The output detection logic uses:
output_ids = [n["id"] for n in model_params["nodes"] if n["id"] not in source_to_targets]

This incorrectly includes custominput nodes as outputs when they 
have no outgoing edges, silently producing a malformed Keras model.

**To Reproduce**

1. Open TensorMap at localhost:3300
2. Create a new project
3. Add an Input node and a Dense node on the canvas
4. Do NOT connect them with an edge
5. Save the model and click Train
6. Backend crashes with KeyError — no error message shown to user

**Expected behavior**
A clear, descriptive error message should tell the user exactly 
which nodes are disconnected, instead of crashing silently with 
a KeyError.

**Screenshots**
Not applicable — bug occurs in backend logic during model generation.

**Environment:**
OS: macOS (latest)
Browser: Chrome (latest)
Node.js version: 18+
Python version: 3.12+

**Additional context**
Affected file: tensormap-backend/app/services/model_generation.py
Affected lines: 65-70

Proposed fix:

# After BFS loop — validate all nodes were visited
unvisited_non_input = {
    nid for nid in nodes_by_id
    if nid not in visited
    and nodes_by_id[nid]["type"] != "custominput"
}
if unvisited_non_input:
    raise ValueError(
        f"Disconnected nodes detected: {unvisited_non_input}. "
        f"Please connect all layers before generating the model."
    )

# Fix output detection to exclude input nodes
output_ids = [
    n["id"] for n in model_params["nodes"]
    if n["id"] not in source_to_targets
    and n["type"] != "custominput"
]

Directly related to GSoC 2026 model generation reliability goal.
